title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of Q1'26
content:
First Lien Real Estate's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances grew 1.5% from the previous period. Total loans of $183,111 thousand remain concentrated in First Lien Real Estate ($13,107 thousand) and Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit Real Estate Secured ($117,072 thousand), which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk.

Portfolio Composition
First Lien Real Estate's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in First Lien Real Estate. As of Q1'26, First Lien Real Estate totaled $13,107 (7.2%) and Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit Real Estate Secured totaled $117,072 (63.9%), together representing approximately 71.1% of the total portfolio.

Portfolio Composition
Used Vehicle totaled $22,831 (12.5%), while New Vehicle accounted for $11,009 (6.0%). Overall loan balances grew 1.5% from the previous period, reflecting continued balance-sheet expansion.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains manageable despite modest delinquency levels within certain segments. Total delinquent loans increased to a figure not explicitly stated in thousands but visible as non-zero values across categories like First Lien Real Estate and Used Vehicle. The largest delinquency exposure remains in Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit Not Real Estate Secured ($410) and All Other Unsecured ($2,652), followed by Junior Lien Real Estate ($12,188).

Delinquency & Credit Quality
The concentration of delinquencies within First Lien Real Estate and Used Vehicle remains consistent with the overall portfolio composition. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
